Search the codebase for these test files mentioned in the plan and find any other test files that assert on label values ("erk-pr",) or PLANS_VIEW or label_code_pr:
1. tests/tui/views/test_types.py 2. tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_label_code_pr.py 3. tests/unit/objective_issues/test_label_definitions.py 4. tests/unit/core/test_health_checks_plans_repo_labels.py
Also search for any test that references the default labels ("erk-pr",) for list_cmd or dash_data.
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
Return the full paths of all matching test files and the key assertions that need to be updated.

1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. Add docs here for planning workflow patterns. 29→- [pr-operations/](pr-operations/) 30→- [refactoring/](refactoring/)

session.assistant: Let me read the most relevant documentation file about label renaming:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: CI Label Rename Checklist 3→read_when: 4→ - renaming a GitHub label used in CI automation 5→ - updating label references across the codebase 6→ - debugging why CI label checks aren't working after a rename 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "Renaming a GitHub label used in CI automation" 9→ warning: "Labels are referenced in multiple places: (1) Job-level if: conditions in all workflow files, (2) Step name descriptions and comments, (3) Documentation examples showing the label check. Missing any location will cause CI behavior to diverge from intent. Use the CI Label Rename Checklist to ensure comprehensive updates." 10→ score: 6 11→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 12→audit_result: clean 13→--- 14→ 15→# CI Label Rename Checklist 16→ 17→## Why Label Renames Are Dangerous 18→ 19→GitHub Actions label checks use **string literals in YAML that cannot be validated statically**. Unlike code references that fail at compile time, label mismatches fail silently: 20→ 21→- Boolean expressions evaluate to `false` when label names don't match 22→- No errors are thrown at workflow parse time or runtime 23→- PRs with the label run CI when they should skip (or vice versa) 24→- No obvious indicators point to the mismatch 25→ 26→The problem: **labels connect Python constants, YAML workflow conditions, and prose documentation across three different file formats with zero static validation**. 27→ 28→## The Coordination Challenge 29→ 30→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/constants.py, PLAN_REVIEW_LABEL -->
... [94 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: PR and Plan Label Assignment Scheme 3→read_when: 4→ - "understanding how plans, code PRs, and learn issues are labeled" 5→ - "working with erk-pr, erk-plan, erk-core, or erk-learn labels" 6→ - "debugging label filtering in TUI or CLI views" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "querying core erk PRs without using the erk-core label" 9→ warning: "Use erk-core to query non-learn PRs (plans + code). Use erk-pr for ALL erk-submitted PRs including learn. Use type-specific labels (erk-plan, erk-learn) only when you need to filter to a specific type." 10→ - action: "backfilling labels on existing issues without considering updated_at side effects" 11→ warning: "GitHub label operations change the issue's updated_at timestamp. This affects sort order in list views and may confuse users." 12→--- 13→ 14→# PR and Plan Label Assignment Scheme 15→ 16→Erk uses `erk-pr` as the **universal base label** on all erk-submitted PRs, with `erk-core` for non-learn PRs and **type-specific labels** for filtering. 17→ 18→## Label Taxonomy 19→ 20→| Label | Purpose | Applied To | 21→| ------------------ | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------- | 22→| `erk-pr` | Base label — all erk-submitted PRs | All plans, learn plans, and code PRs | 23→| `erk-core` | Core work label — non-learn PRs | Plan PRs and code PRs (NOT learn PRs) | 24→| `erk-planned-pr` | Legacy base label — identifies plans | Historical plans only (no longer applied by code) | 25→| `erk-plan` | Type label — implementation plans | Regular plans | 26→| `erk-learn` | Type label — documentation plans | Learn plans | 27→| `erk-objective` | Separate system — objectives | Objective issues | 28→| `erk-consolidated` | Consolidation marker | Consolidated learn plans | 29→ 30→## Label Assignment
... [49 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Tests for check_plans_repo_labels health check. 2→ 3→These tests verify the health check correctly reports label status in the plans repository. 4→Uses FakeGitHubIssues to test label checking behavior. 5→ 6→Note: The doctor check only verifies erk-plan and erk-objective labels. 7→erk-extraction is optional (for documentation workflows) and not checked. 8→""" 9→ 10→from tests.test_utils.paths import sentinel_path 11→ 12→from erk.core.health_checks.plans_repo_labels import check_plans_repo_labels 13→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues 14→ 15→ 16→def test_check_returns_passed_when_all_required_labels_exist() -> None: 17→ """Test that check returns success when required erk labels exist.""" 18→ github_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(labels={"erk-pr", "erk-plan", "erk-objective"}) 19→ 20→ result = check_plans_repo_labels( 21→ repo_root=sentinel_path(), 22→ plans_repo="owner/plans-repo", 23→ github_issues=github_issues, 24→ ) 25→ 26→ assert result.passed is True 27→ assert result.name == "plans-repo-labels" 28→ assert "configured" in result.message.lower() 29→ assert "owner/plans-repo" in result.message 30→

Perfect! I can see the issue clearly now. The PLANS_VIEW currently has `labels=("erk-core",)` on line 41, but the test on line 36 of test_types.py expects it to be `("erk-pr",)`. Let me compile my complete findings:
## Summary
Based on my thorough analysis of the codebase, here are all the test files that assert on label values and will need to be updated when the `erk-core` label scheme is introduced:
### Key Finding
Looking at the plan documentation and code: - **Current state**: PLANS_VIEW uses `"erk-core"` as the label (in `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/views/types.py` line 41) - **Test expectation**: The test in `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/tui/views/test_types.py` line 36 expects `("erk-pr",)`
The plan is to introduce the `erk-core` label for non-learn PRs, which means we need to update the test assertion.
### Test Files Needing Updates
#### 1. **PRIMARY TEST FILE (Must Update)** **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/tui/views/test_types.py` - **Line 36**: `assert PLANS_VIEW.labels == ("erk-pr",)` - **Update to**: `assert PLANS_VIEW.labels == ("erk-core",)` - **Key assertion**: Tests that PLANS_VIEW uses the correct label tuple for querying PRs
#### 2. **LABEL DEFINITION TEST** **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/objective_issues/test_label_definitions.py` - **Lines 58-68**: Test `test_get_erk_label_definitions_contains_erk_pr()` - Asserts `erk_pr.description == "Plan managed as a draft PR"` - **Status**: No changes needed (tests label properties, not usage in views) - **Lines 106-111**: Test `test_get_required_erk_labels_contains_erk_pr()` - Asserts `"erk-pr" in label_names` - **Status**: No changes needed (tests presence of label definition)
#### 3. **HEALTH CHECK TEST** **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/core/test_health_checks_plans_repo_labels.py` - **Lines 16-29**: Test `test_check_returns_passed_when_all_required_labels_exist()` - Creates FakeGitHubIssues with `{"erk-pr", "erk-plan", "erk-objective"}` - **Status**: No changes needed (checks health of label setup, not view filtering) - **Lines 32-45**: Test `test_check_returns_failed_when_one_label_missing()` - Tests with missing `erk-objective` - **Status**: No changes needed - **Lines 48-63**: Test `test_check_returns_failed_when_all_labels_missing()` - Asserts specific labels appear in error messages - **Status**: No changes needed
#### 4. **LABEL CODE PR TEST** **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_label_code_pr.py` - **Line 57**: `assert (42, "erk-pr") in fake_local_github.added_labels` - **Status**: No changes needed - code PRs should still get `erk-pr` label (based on documentation showing code PRs apply `erk-pr` and `erk-core`) - **However**: May need to add assertion for `erk-core` if the implementation adds both labels
#### 5. **PLAN LIST SERVICE TESTS** **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/services/test_plan_list_service.py` - **Lines 53, 130, 152, 194, 209, 261, 289, 314, 353, 425, 463**: Various assertions with `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` or similar - **Context**: These create test data with specific label combinations - **Status**: These are test data setup, not label assertions. Need review based on context of what's being tested.
#### 6. **PLAN DATA PROVIDER TESTS** **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/tui/data/test_provider.py` - **Lines 1833, 1845, 1869, 1881**: Test data setup with `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - **Status**: These are test data setup, not assertions about what labels should be used
#### 7. **VIEW SWITCHING TESTS** **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/tui/app/test_view_switching.py` - **Lines 48, 81, 107, 132, 361, 400**: Reference `("erk-learn",)` and `("erk-objective",)` labels - **Status**: No changes needed (these are correct) - **Line 350**: `plans_labels = get_view_config(ViewMode.PLANS).labels` - **Status**: No changes needed if PLANS_VIEW label is updated in types.py
#### 8. **PLANNED PR BACKEND TEST** **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/plan_store/test_planned_pr_backend.py` - **Lines 48, 63, 83, 100, 118, 134, 152, 196, 226, 284**: Test data with `labels=("erk-plan",)` or `("erk-pr", "erk-learn")` - **Status**: These are test data setup, not assertions about the view
#### 9. **INIT COMMAND TEST** **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/init/test_create_plans_repo_labels.py` - **Lines 23-26**: Test `test_create_plans_repo_labels_creates_all_labels()` - Tests that all five erk labels are created - **Status**: No changes needed
### Commands/PR Tests **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py` - **Lines 1369, 1519, 1533**: Test data with specific label combinations - **Status**: These are test setup, not assertions
---
## Critical Test That MUST Be Updated
The **ONLY test assertion that directly contradicts the plan** is:
**`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/tui/views/test_types.py:36`** ```python assert PLANS_VIEW.labels == ("erk-pr",) ```
This needs to change to: ```python assert PLANS_VIEW.labels == ("erk-core",) ```
Because the source file (`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/views/types.py:41`) already has: ```python labels=("erk-core",), ```
All other test files use labels as test data setup or are testing label definitions themselves, which are correct and need no changes.
